# Harms by Stakeholder Table

| Stakeholder | Harm | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Consumers | Misled by fake proof or hidden incentives | fake reviews, edited testimonials, hidden sponsorship |
| Creators | Crowded out by coordinated volume or copycats | clipping farms, repost networks, impersonation |
| Businesses | Optimize toward fake leads or stolen attribution | invalid traffic, click flooding, fake installs |
| Investors | Infer maturity from inflated or weak metrics | ARR inflation, launch-badge theatre |
| Platforms | Ranking systems learn from corrupted signals | fake engagement, review fraud, app-store discovery fraud |
| Workers | Hidden labor produces “organic” success without visibility | clippers, editors, outsourced moderation, VAs |
| Public trust | Audiences become unable to distinguish culture from campaign | trend simulation, native astroturfing, synthetic influencers |
